name: KeyCorp

profile.description: KeyCorp is a regional bank with 17,469 employees. Q1 2026 net income of $486M ($0.44 EPS, up 33% YoY), revenue of $1.95B (up 10%), NIM of 2.87%. CET1 ratio of 11.4%.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Q1 2026 EPS of $0.44 up 33% YoY with revenue of $1.95B up 10% [1]. NIM of 2.87% expanding. AI Deployment (1/2): Digital banking tools with some AI enhancement. Market Share (1/2): Top 20 US bank. Product Pipeline (0/2): Standard banking products. R&D Intensity (1/1): Technology investment above peer average.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Community banking purpose. Community Impact (1/2): KeyBank Foundation community development programs. Employee Retention (1/2): 17,469 employees with stable leadership. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Q1 2026 EPS up 33%, revenue up 10%, NIM of 2.87% expanding [1]. Forward P/E of 10.05, dividend yield of 3.78% with 25 years paid and 10 years growth. ROE of 9.98% moderate. CET1 of 11.4% strong.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Banking regulation provides defense but fintech AI competition threatens margins. AI Opportunity (0/1): No quantified AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: KeyCorp is a well-managed regional bank with consistent dividend growth and community focus, demonstrating some but not exceptional infinite game characteristics.

investment_thesis: KeyCorp's Q1 2026 showed strong momentum — EPS up 33%, revenue up 10%, NIM expanding to 2.87%, and CET1 at 11.4% [1]. At 10.05x forward earnings with 3.78% dividend yield and 10 consecutive years of growth, KEY offers regional bank value.
thesis_continued: ROE of 9.98% below target [1]. Rate sensitivity means NIM could compress. Regional bank concentration limits growth optionality. Share repurchases of $389M signal confidence but reduce capital flexibility.
key_catalysts: Net interest margin expansion continuing as rate environment supports higher NIM
Fee income diversification through wealth management and capital markets
Cost efficiency improvements driving positive operating leverage
key_risks: Interest rate decline compressing NIM below 2.87% current level
Commercial real estate exposure in key markets creating credit risk
Regional bank M&A pressures as larger banks seek scale acquisitions
sources: KeyCorp Q1 2026 Earnings Release — SEC 8-K Filing, April 2026
Yahoo Finance — KEY Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
